subject: physics
title: Constant Volume Gas Pressure
summary: Reference material for explaining pressure changes when gas temperature rises at constant volume.
lecture_text: When volume is fixed, increasing gas temperature makes particles move faster. Faster particles collide with the container walls more often and with greater force, so pressure increases.
visual_hints: Draw a rigid container before and after heating.
Use longer motion arrows for hotter gas particles.

examples.question: A sealed rigid tank is heated. What happens to pressure?
